title: 306A Plumber Practice Exam 1
description: 150 questions covering all Red Seal 306A Plumber NOA blocks. Based on NPC 2025.

questions.question: According to the NPC 2025, drainage pipes that are NPS 3 or less shall have a minimum downward slope of:
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.4.8.1.(1), drainage pipes NPS 3 or less shall have a downward slope in the direction of flow of at least 1 in 50, which equals approximately 2% or 1/4 inch per foot.

questions.question: What is the minimum nominal pipe size of a fixture outlet pipe for a water closet?
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Table 2.4.9.3, a water closet (both flush tank and direct flush valve types) requires a minimum fixture outlet pipe size of NPS 3.

questions.question: According to Table 2.4.9.3 of the NPC, what is the hydraulic load (in fixture units) for a water closet with a flush tank?
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Table 2.4.9.3, a water closet with a flush tank has a hydraulic load of 4 fixture units. A water closet with a direct flush valve has a higher load of 6 fixture units.

questions.question: According to NPC Table 2.4.9.3, what is the hydraulic load for a bathtub (with or without shower)?
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Table 2.4.9.3, a bathtub (with or without shower) has a hydraulic load of 1½ fixture units and requires a minimum NPS 1½ fixture outlet pipe.

questions.question: The maximum developed length of a fixture outlet pipe shall not exceed:
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.4.8.2.(1), the developed length of fixture outlet pipes shall not exceed 1 200 mm, except for fixture outlet pipes installed in conformance with island plumbing fixture installations.

questions.question: A sanitary drainage pipe shall be of a nominal pipe size not less than:
options: A) The largest trap it serves
B) The largest vent pipe that is connected to it, or the largest sanitary drainage pipe that drains into it
D) One NPS smaller than the pipe it connects to
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.4.9.1.(1), a sanitary drainage pipe shall be of a nominal pipe size not less than a vent pipe connected to it, or the largest sanitary drainage pipe that drains into it. There shall be no reduction in size.

questions.question: What is the minimum size of a building drain and building sewer connected to the public sewer system downstream of the main cleanout?
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.4.9.4.(1), building drains and building sewers connected to the public sewer system downstream of the main cleanout shall be not less than NPS 4.

questions.question: Drainage pipes that serve a water closet shall be not less than:
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.4.9.2.(1), drainage pipes that serve a water closet shall be not less than NPS 3.

questions.question: According to the NPC, what temperature must sewage or clear-water waste be cooled to before being discharged to a drainage system?
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.4.4.2.(1), where a plumbing fixture discharges sewage or clear-water waste at a temperature above 75°C, provision shall be made for cooling the waste to 75°C or less before discharge to the drainage system.

questions.question: One trap is permitted to serve how many compartments of a sink?
options: B) Up to 2 or 3 compartments of a single sink
D) Any number if they are in the same room
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.4.5.1.(2), one trap is permitted to protect all the trays or compartments of a 2- or 3-compartment sink, a 2-compartment laundry tray, or 2 similar single compartment plumbing fixtures in the same room.

questions.question: According to NPC Table 2.4.9.3, what is the hydraulic load for a drinking fountain?
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Table 2.4.9.3, a drinking fountain has a hydraulic load of ½ fixture unit and requires a minimum NPS 1¼ fixture outlet pipe.

questions.question: According to the NPC, no other plumbing fixture shall be connected to:
options: A) A branch downstream of a grease interceptor
B) A lead bend or stub that serves a water closet
C) A fixture drain serving a bathtub
D) A wet vent pipe serving a lavatory
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.4.2.1.(4), no other plumbing fixture shall be connected to a lead bend or stub that serves a water closet.

questions.question: According to NPC Table 2.4.9.3, what is the hydraulic load for a domestic type clothes washer?
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Table 2.4.9.3, a domestic type clothes washer has a hydraulic load of 2 fixture units.

questions.question: According to the NPC, the upper end of every stack shall terminate in a:
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.5.4.1.(1), the upper end of every stack shall terminate in a stack vent.

questions.question: Vent pipes shall be installed without depressions in which:
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.5.6.1.(1), vent pipes shall be installed without depressions in which moisture can collect.

questions.question: According to the NPC, a cleanout fitting shall be provided on the upstream side and directly over every:
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.4.7.1.(2), a cleanout fitting shall be provided on the upstream side and directly over every running trap.

questions.question: What is the hydraulic load for a water closet with a direct flush valve according to NPC Table 2.4.9.3?
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Table 2.4.9.3, a water closet with a direct flush valve has a hydraulic load of 6 fixture units, which is higher than the 4 fixture units assigned to a flush tank water closet.

questions.question: According to NPC Table 2.4.9.3, what is the hydraulic load for a bathroom group with a flush tank?
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Table 2.4.9.3, a bathroom group with a flush tank has a hydraulic load of 6 fixture units. A bathroom group with a direct flush valve has 8 fixture units.

questions.question: What is the maximum hydraulic load (in fixture units) permitted on an NPS 3 stack according to Table 2.4.10.6.-A?
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Table 2.4.10.6.-A, an NPS 3 stack has a maximum permitted hydraulic load of 102 fixture units, with a maximum of 18 fixture units drained from any one storey.

questions.question: According to Table 2.4.10.6.-A, what is the maximum number of fixture units that can be drained from any one storey to an NPS 4 stack?
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Table 2.4.10.6.-A, an NPS 4 stack permits a maximum of 100 fixture units drained from any 1 storey, while the total maximum hydraulic load on the stack is 540 fixture units.

questions.question: What is the maximum hydraulic load (in fixture units) permitted on an NPS 3 branch according to Table 2.4.10.6.-B?
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Table 2.4.10.6.-B, an NPS 3 branch has a maximum permitted hydraulic load of 27 fixture units.

questions.question: According to the NPC, the hydraulic load from a fixture that produces a continuous flow is calculated at how many fixture units per litre per second?
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.4.10.3.(1), the hydraulic load from a fixture that produces a continuous flow is 31.7 fixture units for each litre per second of flow.

questions.question: When the number of fixture units is 260 or fewer and needs to be converted to litres, the hydraulic load is:
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.4.10.5.(1), when the number of fixture units is 260 or fewer, the load is 2 360 L. When the number exceeds 260, the load is 9.1 L for each fixture unit.

questions.question: According to NPC Table 2.4.10.2., what is the hydraulic load from a plumbing fixture with an NPS 2 trap that is not listed in Table 2.4.9.3?
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Table 2.4.10.2., the hydraulic load from a plumbing fixture with an NPS 2 trap is 3 fixture units. This table is used when the fixture is not listed in Table 2.4.9.3.

questions.question: Building drains shall be provided with a cleanout fitting of what minimum size, located as close as practical to where the building drain leaves the building?
options: D) Same size as the building drain
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.4.7.1.(6), building drains shall be provided with a cleanout fitting of NPS 4 or larger, located as close as practical to the place where the building drain leaves the building.

questions.question: According to Table 2.4.7.2, what is the maximum spacing of cleanouts for one-way rodding on NPS 3 and NPS 4 drainage pipes?
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Table 2.4.7.2, for NPS 3 and NPS 4 drainage pipes, the maximum spacing for one-way rodding cleanouts is 15 m, and for two-way rodding is 30 m.

questions.question: Where a cleanout is required on a building sewer of NPS 8 or larger, it shall be a:
options: B) Y fitting with cleanout plug
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.4.7.1.(4), where a cleanout is required on a building sewer of NPS 8 or larger, it shall be a manhole.

questions.question: The piping between a cleanout fitting and the drainage system it serves shall not change direction by more than:
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.4.7.4.(4), the piping between a cleanout fitting and the drainage system it serves shall not change direction by more than 45°.

questions.question: Stacks shall be provided with a cleanout fitting at the bottom of the stack, or not more than what distance upstream of the bottom of the stack?
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.4.7.1.(7), stacks shall be provided with a cleanout fitting at the bottom of the stack, not more than 3 m upstream of the bottom of the stack, or on a Y fitting connecting the stack to the building drain or branch.

questions.question: An interceptor with an effective water seal of not less than what depth is permitted to serve as a trap?
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.4.5.1.(5), an interceptor with an effective water seal of not less than 38 mm is permitted to serve as a trap.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT listed as an acceptable means of maintaining the trap seal of a floor drain?
options: A) Use of a trap seal primer
B) Using the drain as a receptacle for an indirectly connected drinking fountain
C) Installing an air admittance valve on the trap
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.4.5.6.(1), trap seal maintenance options include a trap seal primer, using the drain as a receptacle for an indirectly connected drinking fountain, or other equally effective means. An air admittance valve is not listed as a method for maintaining trap seals.

questions.question: Where a plumbing fixture discharges sewage that includes fats, oils or grease in a restaurant, it shall discharge through a:
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.4.4.3.(1), where a plumbing fixture discharges sewage including fats, oils or grease in a public kitchen, restaurant, or care occupancy, it shall discharge through a grease interceptor.

questions.question: A vent pipe terminal in outside air must be located not less than what distance above ground?
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.5.6.5.(4)(c), where a vent pipe is terminated in outside air, the terminal shall be located not less than 2 m above ground.

questions.question: A vent pipe terminal in outside air must be located not less than what distance from every air inlet, openable window or door?
options: A) Not less than 500 mm above and 2 m in any direction
B) Not less than 1 m above and not less than 3.5 m in any other direction
C) Not less than 2 m above and 5 m in any other direction
D) Not less than 150 mm above and 1 m in any other direction
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.5.6.5.(4)(a), where a vent pipe is terminated in outside air, the terminal shall be located not less than 1 m above and not less than 3.5 m in any other direction from every air inlet, openable window or door.

questions.question: Where a vent pipe passes through a roof, it shall terminate not less than what height above the roof?
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.5.6.5.(5)(a), where a vent pipe passes through a roof, it shall be terminated not less than 150 mm above the roof or above the surface of storm water which could pond on the roof.

questions.question: According to Table 2.5.7.1, what is the minimum vent pipe size for a trap served by an NPS 3 trap?
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Table 2.5.7.1, the minimum vent pipe size for a trap of NPS 3 is NPS 1½.

questions.question: According to Table 2.5.6.3, what is the maximum length of a trap arm for an NPS 2 trap?
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Table 2.5.6.3, the maximum length of a trap arm for an NPS 2 trap served is 2.4 m, with a minimum slope of 1/50.

questions.question: The developed length of a trap arm must be not less than:
options: A) One times the NPS of the fixture drain
B) Twice the NPS of the fixture drain
C) Three times the NPS of the fixture drain
D) Four times the NPS of the fixture drain
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.5.6.3.(1)(a), the developed length of the trap arm must be not less than twice the NPS of the fixture drain. This prevents crown venting of the trap.

questions.question: A wet vent serving water closets is limited to a maximum of how many wet-vented water closets?
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.5.2.1.(1)(b), the number of wet-vented water closets shall not exceed 2.

questions.question: In a wet-vented system, the water closets must be installed in what position relative to other plumbing fixtures?
options: A) Upstream of all other plumbing fixtures
B) Downstream of all other plumbing fixtures
C) Adjacent to but not above other plumbing fixtures
D) At the same elevation as all other plumbing fixtures
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.5.2.1.(1)(d), in a wet-vented system, water closets shall be installed downstream of all other plumbing fixtures.

questions.question: In a wet vent system, trap arms and fixture drains connected to the wet vent shall not exceed what size?
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.5.2.1.(1)(e), trap arms and fixture drains connected to the wet vent shall not exceed NPS 2, except for connections from emergency floor drains in accordance with Sentence 2.5.1.1.(3).

questions.question: Sanitary building drains shall be provided with at least one vent that is not less than:
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.5.7.2.(2), sanitary building drains shall be provided with at least one vent that is not less than NPS 3.

questions.question: Where a vent pipe passes through a roof and may be subject to frost closure, it shall be increased to at least what size before penetrating the roof?
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.5.6.5.(6)(a), where a vent pipe passes through a roof and may be subject to frost closure, it shall be increased at least one NPS, but not less than NPS 3, immediately before it penetrates the roof.

questions.question: Air admittance valves shall be located not less than what distance above the fixture drain being vented?
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.5.9.2.(2)(a), air admittance valves shall be located not less than 100 mm above the fixture drain being vented.

questions.question: The connection of a sanitary drainage pipe to a nominally horizontal drainage pipe or offset shall be not less than what distance from the bottom of a stack that receives 30 or more fixture units?
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.4.2.1.(3), the connection of a sanitary drainage pipe to a nominally horizontal pipe or offset shall be not less than 1.5 m measured horizontally from the bottom of a stack that receives 30 or more fixture units.

questions.question: Every stack that drains plumbing fixtures from more than how many storeys containing plumbing fixtures shall have a vent stack?
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.5.4.2.(1), every stack that drains plumbing fixtures from more than 4 storeys containing plumbing fixtures shall have a vent stack, except that a stack serving as a wet vent does not require a vent stack.

questions.question: Where a stack receives the discharge from plumbing fixtures located on more than how many storeys, a yoke vent must be installed?
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.5.4.3.(1), where a stack receives discharge from plumbing fixtures located on more than 11 storeys, a yoke vent shall be installed for each section of 5 storeys or part thereof counted from the top down.

questions.question: A stack with a nominally horizontal offset more than 1.5 m long requires offset relief vents when the upper vertical portion passes through more than 2 storeys and receives a hydraulic load of more than:
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.5.4.4.(1), offset relief vents are required when a stack has a horizontal offset more than 1.5 m long, the upper vertical portion passes through more than 2 storeys, and receives a hydraulic load of more than 100 fixture units.

questions.question: The minimum nominal pipe size of a vent stack or stack vent shall be what fraction of the NPS of the stack at its base?
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.5.8.4.(3), the minimum nominal pipe size of a vent stack or stack vent shall be one-half the NPS of the stack at its base.

questions.question: In a circuit-vented system, additional circuit vents are required where more than how many circuit-vented plumbing fixtures are connected to a branch between vent pipe connections?
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.5.3.1.(6)(b), additional circuit vents are required where more than 8 circuit-vented plumbing fixtures are connected to a branch between vent pipe connections.

questions.question: A circuit-vented branch, including the fixture drain downstream of the circuit vent connection, shall be not less than what size where traps of NPS 2 or larger are circuit-vented?
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.5.3.1.(9)(b), a circuit-vented branch shall be not less than NPS 3 where traps of NPS 2 or larger are circuit-vented. Where traps less than NPS 2 are circuit-vented, the minimum is NPS 2.

questions.question: A vent pipe that protects a water closet shall be located so that the distance between the fixture drain connection and the vent pipe does not exceed what distance in the horizontal plane?
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.5.6.3.(3), a vent pipe protecting a water closet shall be located so the distance between the fixture drain connection and the vent pipe does not exceed 1 m in the vertical plane and 3 m in the horizontal plane.

questions.question: Every oil interceptor shall be provided with how many vent pipes, and where must they terminate?
options: A) 1 vent pipe, terminating not less than 1 m above ground
B) 2 vent pipes, extending independently to outside air and terminating not less than 2 m above ground
C) 2 vent pipes, connected together and terminating through the roof
D) 1 vent pipe, extending to the vent stack
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.5.5.2.(1), every oil interceptor shall be provided with 2 vent pipes that connect at opposite ends, extend independently to outside air, and terminate not less than 2 m above ground at elevations differing by at least 300 mm.

questions.question: Where a building trap is installed, a fresh air inlet shall be not less than NPS 4 and connected upstream and within what distance of the building trap?
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.5.5.4.(1), where a building trap is installed, a fresh air inlet not less than NPS 4 shall be connected upstream and within 1.2 m of the building trap and downstream of any other connection.

questions.question: According to the NPC, where a wet vent extends through more than one storey, the total discharge from any one storey above the first storey shall not exceed:
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.5.2.1.(1)(h), where a wet vent extends through more than one storey, the total discharge from any one storey above the first storey does not exceed 4 fixture units.

questions.question: According to the NPC, which of the following is an acceptable method to prevent back-siphonage?
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.6.2.2.(2), back-siphonage shall be prevented by devices including an atmospheric vacuum breaker, air gap, pressure vacuum breaker, or other listed devices. A check valve alone or gate valve is not acceptable for back-siphonage prevention.

questions.question: What is the maximum temperature that water discharging from a shower head or into a bathtub shall not exceed, according to the NPC?
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.2.10.7.(4), the temperature of water discharging from a shower head or into a bathtub shall not exceed 49°C, except in healthcare facilities and seniors' residences where the limit is 43°C.

questions.question: According to the NPC, hot water controls on a plumbing fixture shall be on which side?
options: D) Either side, as long as marked
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.6.1.1.(1), plumbing fixtures supplied with separate hot and cold water controls shall have the hot water control on the left and the cold on the right.

questions.question: The temperature-sensing element of a temperature-relief valve on a storage-type service water heater must be located within what distance from the top of the tank?
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.6.1.7.(2)(a), the temperature-sensing element must be located within the top 150 mm of the tank. The valve must be designed to keep water temperature from exceeding 99°C.

questions.question: According to the NPC, the temperature-relief valve on a storage-type water heater must be designed to keep the water temperature from exceeding what temperature?
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.6.1.7.(2)(b), the temperature-relief valve must be designed to open and discharge sufficient water from the tank to keep the temperature of the water in the tank from exceeding 99°C under all operating conditions.

questions.question: According to the NPC, the discharge pipe from a relief valve on a water heater must terminate with an indirect connection above a floor drain with an air break of not more than:
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.6.1.7.(5)(b), discharge pipes connected to a temperature-relief valve or pressure-relief valve shall be rigid, slope downward from the valve, and terminate with an indirect connection above a floor drain, sump, or other safe location, with an air break of not more than 300 mm.

questions.question: What is the minimum nominal pipe size of a water service pipe according to the NPC?
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.6.3.4.(1), water service pipes shall be sized according to the peak demand flow but shall not be less than NPS ¾.

questions.question: According to the NPC, a shut-off valve is required on the water supply pipe to which plumbing fixture?
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.6.1.3.(4), water closets shall be provided with a shut-off valve on their water supply pipe. This is a specific requirement for water closets.

questions.question: Where shall a shut-off valve on a water service pipe be located according to the NPC?
options: B) As close as possible to where the water service pipe enters the building
C) At the water meter location
D) Within 3 m of the first branch connection
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.6.1.3.(1), water service pipes shall be provided with an accessible shut-off valve located as close as possible to where the water service pipe enters the building.

questions.question: In healthcare facilities and seniors' residences, the maximum water temperature discharging from a shower head or into a bathtub shall not exceed:
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.2.10.7.(5)(a), in healthcare facilities and seniors' residences, the temperature of water discharging from a shower head or into a bathtub shall not exceed 43°C and shall be adjusted at the shower or bathtub controls.

questions.question: According to the NPC, a hot water distribution system of a developed length of more than 30 m or supplying more than how many storeys requires temperature maintenance by recirculation or heat tracing?
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.6.1.1.(2), in a hot water distribution system of a developed length of more than 30 m or supplying more than 4 storeys, the water temperature shall be maintained by recirculation or a self-regulating heat tracing system.

questions.question: According to the NPC, what is the thermostat setting required for electric storage-type service water heaters?
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.6.1.12.(1), thermostat controls for electric storage-type service water heaters shall be set at a temperature of 60°C. This is to prevent the growth of legionella bacteria.

questions.question: According to the NPC, backflow caused by back pressure of toxic substances into a potable water system shall be prevented by the installation of:
options: A) A double check valve assembly or an air gap
B) An air gap or a reduced pressure principle backflow preventer
C) A dual check valve backflow preventer only
D) An atmospheric vacuum breaker or a check valve
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.6.2.3.(3), backflow caused by back pressure of toxic substances into a potable water system shall be prevented by the installation of an air gap or a reduced pressure principle backflow preventer. These provide the highest level of protection.

questions.question: According to the NPC, an air gap for backflow prevention shall be not less than 25 mm high and at least how many times the diameter of the water supply outlet opening?
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.6.2.9.(2), air gaps shall be not less than 25 mm high and at least twice the diameter of the opening of the water supply outlet in height.

questions.question: An atmospheric vacuum breaker may be subject to water supply pressure for continuous periods not exceeding:
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.6.2.10.(2)(b), where an atmospheric vacuum breaker is installed, it shall be subject to water supply pressure only when the valve or faucet is open and for periods of continuous use not exceeding 12 hours.

questions.question: According to the NPC, a pressure vacuum breaker or spill-resistant pressure vacuum breaker shall be installed so that the critical level is not less than what distance above the flood level rim?
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.6.2.10.(4), a pressure vacuum breaker or spill-resistant pressure vacuum breaker shall be installed so that the critical level is not less than 300 mm above the flood level rim of a plumbing fixture or tank.

questions.question: An atmospheric vacuum breaker must be installed so that its critical level is at least what minimum distance above the flood level rim?
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.6.2.10.(3), an atmospheric vacuum breaker shall be installed so that the critical level is at least the distance specified by the manufacturer but not less than 25 mm above the flood level rim of a plumbing fixture or tank.

questions.question: According to the NPC, backflow preventers for potable water system connections shall be selected and installed in conformance with which standard?
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.6.2.1.(3), backflow preventers shall be selected and installed in conformance with CSA B64.10/CSA B64.10.1, 'Selection and installation of backflow preventers/Maintenance and field testing of backflow preventers.'

questions.question: According to the NPC, direct flush valves shall be provided with which device to prevent back-siphonage?
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.2.10.8.(1)(d), direct flush valves shall be provided with a vacuum breaker unless the plumbing fixture is designed so that back-siphonage cannot occur.

questions.question: According to the NPC, which of the following shall NOT be installed in a water supply system?
options: C) Bypass piping capable of reducing the effectiveness of a backflow preventer
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.6.2.12.(1), no bypass piping or other device capable of reducing the effectiveness of a backflow preventer shall be installed in a water supply system.

questions.question: According to the NPC, solders and fluxes used in a potable water system shall have a lead content not exceeding:
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.2.9.2.(2), solders and fluxes having a lead content in excess of 0.2% shall not be used in a potable water system.

questions.question: According to the NPC, PVC water pipe and fittings shall NOT be used in which type of system?
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.2.5.7.(4), PVC water pipe and fittings shall not be used in a hot water system. PVC is only suitable for cold water pressure applications.

questions.question: According to the NPC, which type of copper tube is NOT permitted for use in a water distribution system?
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Table 2.2.7.4., Type M soft temper copper tube is not permitted for any plumbing purpose (all entries show 'N'). Type M hard temper is permitted for aboveground water distribution.

questions.question: According to the NPC, cast-iron soil pipe and fittings shall NOT be used in which system?
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.2.6.1.(2), cast-iron soil pipe and fittings shall not be used in a water system. They are intended for drainage and venting applications only.

questions.question: According to the NPC, crosslinked polyethylene (PEX) pipe used in hot and cold potable water systems shall conform to which standard?
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.2.5.6.(1), crosslinked polyethylene pipe and manufacturer-approved fittings used in hot and cold potable water systems shall conform to CSA B137.5, 'Crosslinked polyethylene (PEX) tubing systems for pressure applications.'

questions.question: According to the NPC, galvanized steel pipe and fittings shall NOT be used in a water distribution system except:
options: B) In buildings of industrial occupancy or for repair of existing galvanized steel piping
D) In buildings not exceeding 3 storeys
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.2.6.7.(3), galvanized steel pipe and fittings shall not be used in a water distribution system except in buildings of industrial occupancy or for the repair of existing galvanized steel piping systems.

questions.question: According to the NPC, which grade(s) of stainless steel pipe shall be used in plumbing systems?
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.2.6.10.(3), only grade 304/304L or 316/316L stainless steel pipe shall be used in plumbing systems.

questions.question: According to the NPC, materials and equipment previously used for a purpose other than distribution of potable water:
options: A) May be reused in a potable water system if cleaned and sanitized
B) Shall not be subsequently used in a potable water system
C) May be reused if approved by the authority having jurisdiction
D) May be reused after pressure testing
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.2.1.2.(1), materials and equipment that have been used for a purpose other than the distribution of potable water shall not be subsequently used in a potable water system.

questions.question: According to the NPC, water hammer arresters shall conform to which standard?
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.2.10.15.(1), water hammer arresters shall conform to ANSI/ASSE 1010, 'Water Hammer Arresters.'

questions.question: According to the NPC, what is the maximum water flow rate for shower heads?
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Table 2.2.10.6., the maximum water flow rate for shower heads is 7.6 L/min. Supply fittings shall have an integral means of limiting the maximum water flow rate to this value.

questions.question: According to the NPC, what is the maximum water flow rate for a private lavatory supply fitting?
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Table 2.2.10.6., the maximum water flow rate for private lavatory supply fittings is 5.7 L/min, while public lavatory supply fittings have a maximum of 1.9 L/min.

questions.question: According to the NPC, PVC water pipe for pressure applications shall have a pressure rating of not less than:
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.2.5.7.(1)(b), PVC water pipe shall have a pressure rating of not less than 1 100 kPa.

questions.question: According to the NPC, polyethylene water pipe shall not be used except for:
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.2.5.4.(2), polyethylene water pipe shall not be used except for a water service pipe. It is restricted to this specific application.

questions.question: According to Table 2.3.4.5. of the NPC, what is the maximum horizontal support spacing for cast-iron pipe?
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Table 2.3.4.5., the maximum horizontal spacing of supports for cast-iron pipe is 3.0 m, with the additional condition that support shall be at or adjacent to each hub or joint.

questions.question: According to the NPC, what is the maximum horizontal support spacing for PEX plastic pipe?
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Table 2.3.4.5., the maximum horizontal spacing of supports for PEX plastic pipe is 0.8 m.

questions.question: According to the NPC, what is the maximum horizontal support spacing for CPVC pipe?
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Table 2.3.4.5., the maximum horizontal spacing of supports for CPVC pipe is 1.0 m.

questions.question: According to the NPC, what is the maximum horizontal support spacing for hard temper copper tube with a diameter greater than NPS 1?
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Table 2.3.4.5., the maximum horizontal spacing of supports for hard temper copper tube or copper and brass pipe with a diameter greater than NPS 1 is 3.0 m. For diameter NPS 1 or less, the spacing is 2.5 m.

questions.question: According to the NPC, the maximum vertical spacing for supports on vertical piping shall be:
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.3.4.4.(2), the maximum spacing of supports for vertical piping shall be 7.5 m. Vertical piping shall also be supported at its base and at the floor level of alternate storeys.

questions.question: According to the NPC, every caulked lead drainage joint shall be firmly packed with oakum and tightly caulked with lead to a depth of not less than:
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.3.2.1.(2), every caulked lead drainage joint shall be firmly packed with oakum and tightly caulked with lead to a depth of not less than 25 mm.

questions.question: According to the NPC, flared joints shall NOT be used for which type of copper tube?
options: D) Type K hard temper underground
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.3.2.5.(2), flared joints shall not be used for hard (drawn) copper tube. Flared joints are only suitable for soft temper copper tube.

questions.question: According to the NPC, joints in copper tubes installed underground shall be made with flared or compression fittings, or be brazed using a brazing alloy within which range?
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.3.3.12.(1), joints in copper tubes installed underground shall be made with flared or compression fittings, or be brazed using a brazing alloy within the American Welding Society's AWS-BCuP range.

questions.question: According to the NPC, compression fittings shall NOT be used underground in which location?
options: D) In a water service trench
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.3.3.12.(2), compression fittings shall not be used underground under a building.

questions.question: According to the NPC, Types M and DWV copper tube shall NOT be:
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.3.3.10.(1), Types M and DWV copper tube shall not be bent. Only Types K and L copper tube in soft temper may be bent.

questions.question: When making a screwed joint in a pipe, what must be done to the pipe ends before assembly?
options: B) Reamed or filed out to the size of the bore with all chips removed
D) Coated with flux on all surfaces
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.3.2.3.(1), in making a screwed joint, the ends of the pipe shall be reamed or filed out to the size of the bore and all chips and cuttings shall be removed.

questions.question: According to the NPC, where a hanger or support for copper tube is of a material other than brass or copper, it shall be:
options: B) Suitably separated and electrically insulated from the pipe or tube
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.3.4.3.(1), where a hanger or support for copper tube or brass or copper pipe is of a material other than brass or copper, it shall be suitably separated and electrically insulated from the pipe or tube to prevent galvanic corrosion.

questions.question: According to the NPC, at what pressure shall an air pressure test be conducted on a drainage or venting system?
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.3.6.5.(1)(a), air shall be forced into the system until a pressure of 35 kPa is created, and this pressure shall be maintained for at least 15 minutes without a drop in pressure.

questions.question: According to the NPC, a potable water system shall be able to withstand an air pressure test of not less than 700 kPa for what duration?
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.3.7.2.(1)(b), potable water systems shall be able to withstand an air pressure of not less than 700 kPa for at least 2 hours without a drop in pressure.

questions.question: According to the NPC, what shall be used to test a potable water system when performing a water pressure test?
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.3.7.3.(2), potable water shall be used to test a potable water system. This prevents contamination of the system during testing.

questions.question: According to the NPC, during a final test on a drainage or venting system, a pressure equivalent to what water column shall be maintained for 15 minutes?
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.3.6.6.(1)(e), during a final test, a pressure equivalent to a 25 mm water column shall be maintained for 15 minutes without the addition of more smoke.

questions.question: According to the NPC, where static pressure at any plumbing fixture may exceed what value, a pressure-reducing valve shall be installed?
questions.explanation: Per NPC 2025 Section 2.6.3.3.(1), where the static pressure at any plumbing fixture may exceed 550 kPa, a pressure-reducing valve shall be installed to limit the maximum static pressure at the plumbing fixture to 550 kPa.

questions.question: What colour of WHMIS border on a Safety Data Sheet (SDS) pictogram indicates a health hazard such as carcinogenicity or respiratory sensitization?
options: B) Red diamond with white background
C) Red diamond with a person silhouette symbol
questions.explanation: Under WHMIS 2015 (GHS), the health hazard pictogram featuring a person silhouette within a red diamond border indicates serious health hazards such as carcinogenicity, respiratory sensitization, and organ toxicity.

questions.question: According to Ontario's Occupational Health & Safety Act, a worker has the right to refuse work that they believe is likely to endanger themselves or another worker. This is known as the right to:
questions.explanation: The OH&S Act establishes three fundamental worker rights: the right to know about hazards, the right to participate in health and safety decisions, and the right to refuse unsafe work.

questions.question: When working at heights above 3 metres (10 feet), what personal protective equipment is required by Ontario regulations?
options: B) Full body harness with lanyard tied off to an adequate anchor
C) Safety glasses and steel-toed boots
questions.explanation: Ontario's Working at Heights regulation requires workers to use a full body harness connected to a lifeline or adequate anchor point when working at heights where a fall hazard exists, generally at 3 metres or more.

questions.question: A plumber is rigging a cast iron bathtub weighing 180 kg for a crane lift. The sling has a Working Load Limit (WLL) of 500 kg at a 90° included angle. What is the approximate capacity of the same sling at a 60° included angle?
questions.explanation: Sling capacity decreases as the included angle decreases. At 60° the capacity factor is approximately 0.866 (cos 30°) of the rated capacity at 0° — but since the 500 kg rating is at 90°, the 60° capacity is 500 × sin(60°)/sin(90°) ≈ 433 kg. The reduction factor compared to 90° is approximately 0.866.

questions.question: Under WHMIS, a Safety Data Sheet (SDS) must contain how many sections?
questions.explanation: Under WHMIS 2015 (aligned with GHS), a Safety Data Sheet must contain exactly 16 sections covering identification, hazards, composition, first aid, firefighting, accidental release, handling, exposure controls, physical/chemical properties, stability, toxicology, ecology, disposal, transport, regulatory, and other information.

questions.question: A plumber needs to calculate the travel length of a 45° offset connecting two parallel pipes that are 12 inches apart (centre to centre). Using the constant for a 45° offset, what is the travel length?
questions.explanation: For a 45° offset, the travel = offset distance × 1.414 (which is √2). So 12 × 1.414 = 16.97 inches. The constant 1.414 is the multiplier for 45° fittings.

questions.question: On a plumbing isometric drawing, a fitting drawn as a short line at 45° to the pipe run with a dot at the connection point typically represents a:
questions.explanation: In isometric plumbing drawings, a 45° elbow is represented by a short diagonal line showing the change in direction at 45°. A tee is shown as a branch off the main line, while a 90° elbow shows a right-angle direction change.

questions.question: A plumber must run a pipe from one floor to another through a chase, requiring a rolling offset. The horizontal run is 24 inches, the vertical rise is 18 inches, and the lateral offset is 10 inches. Using the Pythagorean theorem applied twice, what is the approximate travel length?
questions.explanation: For a rolling offset, first find the true offset: √(18² + 10²) = √(324 + 100) = √424 ≈ 20.59 inches. Then find the travel: √(20.59² + 24²) = √(423.9 + 576) = √999.9 ≈ 31.6 inches.

questions.question: What class of fire extinguisher should a plumber use on a fire involving energized electrical equipment?
questions.explanation: Class C fire extinguishers are rated for fires involving energized electrical equipment. Class A is for ordinary combustibles, Class B for flammable liquids, and Class D for combustible metals.

questions.question: When excavating a trench deeper than 1.2 metres for underground plumbing, Ontario regulations require:
options: A) Only a safety watch person at the surface
B) Shoring, trench boxes, or sloped walls to prevent cave-in
C) A building permit from the local authority
D) The trench to be filled within 24 hours
questions.explanation: Ontario's Construction Regulations under the OH&S Act require that trenches deeper than 1.2 metres must be protected against cave-in using shoring, trench boxes, or appropriately sloped walls based on soil classification.

questions.question: According to the NPC, what is the definition of a 'bathroom group'?
options: A) Any room containing a water closet and lavatory
B) One lavatory, one water closet, and either one bathtub or one shower
C) Two lavatories, one water closet, and one bathtub
D) One lavatory, two water closets, and one bathtub
questions.explanation: Per NPC 1.4.1.2., a bathroom group is defined as a group of plumbing fixtures installed in the same room, consisting of one domestic-type lavatory, one water closet and either one bathtub (with or without a shower) or one one-head shower.

questions.question: Per the NPC, used plumbing fixtures may be reused only if they:
options: A) Are less than 10 years old
B) Have been inspected by the manufacturer
C) Meet the requirements of the Code for new materials and are satisfactory for their intended use
D) Are the same brand and model as the original installation
questions.explanation: Per NPC 1.2.2.2.(1), used materials and equipment, including plumbing fixtures, shall not be reused unless they meet the requirements of the Code for new materials and equipment and are otherwise satisfactory for their intended use.

questions.question: According to Part 7 of the Ontario Building Code, public washrooms in an office building must be provided with a minimum of one water closet for each:
questions.explanation: OBC Part 7 requires water closets in business and personal service occupancies to be provided at a ratio of approximately 1 per 25 persons of each sex, though exact ratios vary by occupancy type.

questions.question: According to the NPC, a 'flood level rim' is defined as:
options: A) The overflow opening of a fixture
B) The top edge at which water can overflow from a plumbing fixture or device
C) The maximum water level in a water closet tank
D) The height of the trap weir above the floor
questions.explanation: Per NPC 1.4.1.2., the flood level rim is defined as the top edge at which water can overflow from a plumbing fixture or device. This is a critical reference point for determining air gap distances.

questions.question: An accessible water closet in a barrier-free washroom must have its seat height within what range from the finished floor?
questions.explanation: Per OBC barrier-free requirements, accessible water closets must have a seat height between 430 mm and 480 mm from the finished floor to facilitate transfer from a wheelchair.

questions.question: What is the maximum flush volume for a water closet installed in new construction under current Canadian plumbing standards?
questions.explanation: Under current NPC requirements referencing CSA standards, water closets must not exceed 6.0 litres per flush (LPF). Many high-efficiency toilets now use 4.8 LPF or less, but 6.0 LPF is the code maximum for single-flush models.

questions.question: In a public washroom serving a Group A assembly occupancy with a capacity of 300 persons (150 male, 150 female), what is the minimum number of water closets required for the female washroom per OBC Part 7?
questions.explanation: OBC Part 7 Table 7.2.2.2. requires water closets for female patrons in assembly occupancies at a ratio of approximately 1 per 25 female persons. For 150 females, this equals 6 water closets minimum.

questions.question: A shower stall in a residential dwelling must have a minimum interior floor area of:
questions.explanation: The minimum shower receptor size for a residential shower stall is 600 mm × 600 mm (approximately 24" × 24"). Larger sizes are common but this is the code minimum.

questions.question: According to the NPC definition, what distinguishes 'private use' from 'public use' plumbing fixtures?
options: A) Private use fixtures are more expensive commercial grade
B) Private use fixtures are in residences, apartments, and similar installations for one family or an individual
C) Private use fixtures have lower flow rates
D) Private use fixtures require separate venting
questions.explanation: Per NPC 1.4.1.2., private use fixtures are those in residences and apartments, in private washrooms of hotels, and in similar installations in other buildings for one family or an individual. Public use fixtures are those not intended for private use.

questions.question: An interceptor is required in the drainage system when:
options: A) The building has more than 3 storeys
B) Oil, grease, sand, or other harmful materials may enter the drainage system
C) The building drain exceeds NPS 4
D) The fixture is on a branch longer than 3 metres
questions.explanation: Per NPC 1.4.1.2., an interceptor is a receptacle installed to prevent oil, grease, sand, or other materials from passing into the drainage system. They are required where such materials may enter the system.

questions.question: In a closed hydronic heating system, what is the primary purpose of an expansion tank?
options: A) To filter impurities from the heating water
B) To absorb the increased volume of water as it is heated and prevent excessive pressure buildup
C) To store extra water for makeup purposes
D) To separate air from the circulating water
questions.explanation: An expansion tank accommodates the thermal expansion of water as it heats up in a closed system. Without it, the pressure could exceed safe limits and trigger the relief valve or damage components.

questions.question: When sizing a diaphragm-type expansion tank for a hydronic heating system, which of the following factors is NOT required for the calculation?
options: C) Pipe material used in the system
D) System fill pressure and relief valve setting
questions.explanation: Expansion tank sizing depends on total system water volume, the temperature range (from fill to max operating), fill pressure, and relief valve pressure setting. The pipe material does not directly factor into the expansion tank calculation.

questions.question: In a hydronic heating system, the flow check valve (or spring-loaded check valve) installed on each zone is primarily used to:
options: A) Regulate water temperature to each zone
B) Prevent gravity circulation (heat migration) when the zone circulator is off
C) Protect the boiler from thermal shock
D) Balance flow between different zones
questions.explanation: Flow check valves prevent thermosiphoning (gravity circulation) of hot water through zones that are not calling for heat. Without them, heat would migrate to zones even when their circulator is off.

questions.question: A hydronic boiler has a maximum operating temperature of 82°C and a relief valve set at 207 kPa (30 psi). If the system fill pressure is 83 kPa (12 psi) and the total system volume is 400 litres, what is the approximate minimum expansion tank acceptance volume needed?
questions.explanation: Water expands approximately 2.9% when heated from 4°C to 82°C. The expansion volume is 400 × 0.029 = 11.6 litres. With the pressure acceptance factor (fill pressure/relief valve setting), the minimum tank size is approximately 12 litres after accounting for the pressure ratio.

questions.question: What is the minimum relief valve setting typically required on a hot water boiler operating below 121°C?
questions.explanation: Standard hot water boilers operating below 121°C typically require a pressure relief valve with a minimum setting of 207 kPa (30 psi) per CSA B214. The relief valve must be sized to relieve the full boiler output.

questions.question: In a hydronic heating system, a 'primary-secondary' piping arrangement uses closely spaced tees. The recommended maximum distance between the two tees is:
questions.explanation: In primary-secondary hydronic piping, the closely spaced tees should be no more than 12 pipe diameters apart (commonly specified as 4 to 12 diameters) to ensure hydraulic separation between the primary and secondary loops.

questions.question: According to CSA B149.1, black steel pipe used for natural gas piping must be joined by which method?
options: C) Threaded, welded, or flanged connections
questions.explanation: CSA B149.1 requires that black steel gas pipe be joined by threaded connections with approved pipe joint compound or tape, welded joints, or flanged connections. Soldering and solvent cement are not acceptable for gas piping.

questions.question: When sizing natural gas pipe, which of the following factors does NOT affect the pipe size selection?
options: A) Total BTU load of all connected appliances
B) Length of pipe from the meter to the most remote appliance
C) Allowable pressure drop in the system
D) The colour of the pipe
questions.explanation: Gas pipe sizing is based on total BTU/h demand, equivalent pipe length to the most remote appliance, inlet pressure, and allowable pressure drop. The colour of the pipe has no bearing on sizing calculations.

questions.question: According to CSA B149.1, what is the minimum burial depth for gas piping installed underground outside a building?
questions.explanation: CSA B149.1 requires that gas piping installed underground outside a building be buried at a minimum depth of 600 mm (24 inches) below finished grade to protect it from damage.

questions.question: Under CSA B149.1, a Category III gas vent is designed for appliances that produce flue gases with:
options: A) Negative pressure and temperatures below the dew point
B) Positive pressure and temperatures above the dew point
C) Negative pressure and temperatures above the dew point
D) Positive pressure and temperatures below the dew point
questions.explanation: Category III venting handles positive-pressure flue gases with temperatures above the dew point (non-condensing). Category I is negative/non-condensing, Category II is negative/condensing, and Category IV is positive/condensing.

questions.question: A high-efficiency condensing gas furnace (90%+ AFUE) typically requires which category of venting?
options: B) Category II — negative pressure, condensing
C) Category III — positive pressure, non-condensing
D) Category IV — positive pressure, condensing
questions.explanation: High-efficiency condensing furnaces (90%+ AFUE) produce flue gases under positive pressure at temperatures below the dew point, causing condensation. This requires Category IV venting, typically PVC or CPVC pipe rated for the application.

questions.question: A gas drip leg (sediment trap) must be installed ahead of the gas controls on an appliance. What is the minimum length required for the drip leg?
questions.explanation: CSA B149.1 requires a sediment trap (drip leg) with a minimum length of 75 mm (3 inches) to be installed as close as practical to the inlet of the appliance controls to catch debris and moisture before it reaches the gas valve.

questions.question: What is the standard pressure of natural gas supplied to residential appliances in Ontario?
questions.explanation: Standard residential natural gas delivery pressure in Ontario is 1.75 kPa (7 inches water column). This is the common operating pressure for residential appliances. Higher pressures (2 psi) are used for distribution mains.

questions.question: What is the minimum separation distance between a drilled well and a septic tank in Ontario?
questions.explanation: Ontario regulations require a minimum separation distance of 15 metres (approximately 50 feet) between a drilled well and a septic tank. Greater distances are required from the leaching bed.

questions.question: According to the Ontario Building Code, a conventional septic tank serving a residential dwelling must have a minimum liquid capacity of:
options: A) 1,800 litres for dwellings with up to 2 bedrooms
B) 2,500 litres for dwellings with up to 3 bedrooms
C) 3,600 litres for all residential dwellings
D) 4,500 litres for dwellings with up to 4 bedrooms
questions.explanation: OBC Part 8 requires a minimum septic tank capacity of 3,600 litres for residential dwellings. This ensures adequate retention time for the sewage to undergo anaerobic decomposition before effluent reaches the leaching bed.

questions.question: A percolation test (perc test) is performed to determine:
options: A) The pH level of the soil
B) The rate at which water is absorbed by the soil for leaching bed design
C) The depth of bedrock below the surface
questions.explanation: A percolation test measures the rate at which water drains into the soil and is used to determine the soil's ability to absorb septic effluent. The results (T-time in minutes per inch) are used to size the leaching bed absorption area.

questions.question: The minimum separation distance between a leaching bed and a lake or watercourse in Ontario is:
questions.explanation: OBC Part 8 requires a minimum 15 metres separation between a leaching bed and any surface water body such as a lake, river, or watercourse to protect water quality from contamination by septic effluent.

questions.question: According to the NPC, a 'building sewer' begins at what distance from the building wall?
questions.explanation: Per NPC 1.4.1.2., a building sewer is defined as a pipe that is connected to a building drain 1 m outside a wall of a building and that leads to a public sewer or private sewage disposal system.

questions.question: Medical gas piping systems in hospitals must be installed in accordance with which standard?
questions.explanation: CSA Z7396.1 (Medical Gas Pipeline Systems) governs the design, installation, testing, and certification of medical gas piping systems including oxygen, nitrous oxide, medical air, nitrogen, and vacuum systems in healthcare facilities.

questions.question: When brazing medical gas copper piping, the joint must be purged with nitrogen during the brazing process. What is the primary reason for this requirement?
options: A) To keep the pipe cool during brazing
B) To prevent oxide scale formation inside the pipe which could contaminate the medical gas system
C) To reduce the brazing temperature needed
D) To test the joint for leaks during brazing
questions.explanation: Nitrogen purging during brazing of medical gas piping prevents the formation of copper oxide scale inside the pipe. This scale can flake off and contaminate the medical gas system, potentially introducing harmful particles into gases delivered to patients.

questions.question: According to the NPC, a 'leader' is defined as a pipe that:
options: A) Connects a water service pipe to the distribution system
B) Carries storm water from a roof to a storm building drain, sewer, or other place of disposal
C) Extends vertically through one or more storeys in a drainage system
D) Connects a trap to a vent pipe
questions.explanation: Per NPC 1.4.1.2., a leader is defined as a pipe that is installed to carry storm water from a roof to a storm building drain or sewer or other place of disposal.

questions.question: In a storm water drainage system, a flow control roof drain is used to:
options: A) Increase the flow rate of storm water into the leader
B) Prevent backflow of storm water onto the roof
C) Restrict the flow of storm water into the storm drainage system to allow controlled roof ponding
D) Filter debris from storm water before it enters the drain
questions.explanation: Per NPC 1.4.1.2., a flow control roof drain restricts the flow of storm water into the storm drainage system. This allows controlled ponding on the roof to reduce peak flow rates into the drainage system, provided the roof is structurally designed for the load.

questions.question: The National Plumbing Code of Canada 2025 is organized into how many Divisions?
questions.explanation: Per NPC Section 1.3.1., the NPC is organized into three Divisions: Division A (Compliance, Objectives and Functional Statements), Division B (Acceptable Solutions), and Division C (Administrative Provisions).

questions.question: According to the NPC, Division B contains:
options: A) The compliance and application provisions
B) The acceptable solutions of the Code
D) The objectives and functional statements
questions.explanation: Per NPC 1.3.1.2.(1), Division B contains the acceptable solutions of the Code. Division A contains compliance, objectives, and functional statements, while Division C contains administrative provisions.

questions.question: According to the NPC, compliance with the Code can be achieved by complying with the acceptable solutions in Division B, OR by:
options: A) Obtaining a variance from the local building official
B) Using alternative solutions that achieve at least the minimum level of performance required by Division B
C) Following the manufacturer's installation instructions only
D) Meeting the requirements of any international plumbing code
questions.explanation: Per NPC 1.2.1.1.(1), compliance is achieved either by following the acceptable solutions in Division B, or by using alternative solutions that will achieve at least the minimum level of performance required by Division B in the areas defined by the objectives and functional statements.
